article.title: Jeff Mills: Detroit Techno Pioneer and Radio Innovator
article.subtitle: From WJLB's The Wizard to foundational techno architect
article.author: C. Mora
article.excerpt: Jeff Mills emerged in the 1980s as an influential radio DJ on WJLB under the pseudonym The Wizard, spinning eclectic sets that would later inform his contributions to Detroit techno.

content.text: Jeff Mills is a pivotal figure in the development of Detroit techno, whose career began in the 1980s as an influential radio DJ on WJLB under the pseudonym The Wizard.

Mills' sets were a highlight of the nightly show from Charles Johnson, known as The Electrifying Mojo. Complementing Mojo's eclectic playlists, Mills would spin obscure Detroit techno, hip-hop, electro, freestyle, Miami bass, Chicago house, and classic new wave tracks.
His early radio work provided a foundation for his later contributions to electronic music production. Mills is credited with laying the groundwork for the legendary Detroit techno collective.
Mills' work spans genres including techno, minimal, and electronic music, with a strong association to the Detroit techno scene.
Artists similar to Mills include Millsart, Joey Beltram, Circuit Breaker, Robert Hood, and Richie Hawtin.
